content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "collaboration",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 52,
    "domain_depth": 40,
    "data_richness": 35,
    "interaction_quality": 45,
    "ai_integration": 10,
    "mobile_first": 30,
    "performance": 50,
    "typescript_quality": 55,
    "error_handling": 38,
    "realtime": 55,
    "security": 45,
    "differentiation": 35,
    "enterprise_readiness": 40,
    "accessibility": 28
  },
  "overall_score": 41,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "PresenceBar avatars lack ARIA labels and keyboard navigation",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx",
      "description": "Avatar circles have no aria-label on individual elements, no role attributes, and no keyboard focus indicators. Screen readers cannot announce who is currently viewing. Tooltip content is inaccessible via keyboard since avatars are plain divs, not focusable elements.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx, in the avatar rendering section where each user gets a circular div: BEFORE: Each avatar is a plain `<div>` with no role, no tabIndex, and no aria-label. Tooltips only activate on hover. AFTER: Wrap each avatar in a `<button>` element (or add `role=\"img\"` and `tabIndex={0}`) with `aria-label={`${user.displayName} is ${getPresenceStatus(user.lastSeen)}`}`. Ensure the Radix Tooltip trigger is keyboard-focusable so Tab can cycle through avatars. Add a visible focus ring using `outline: 2px solid ${colors.primary}` on `:focus-visible`. The outer presence container should have `role=\"group\"` and `aria-label=\"Team members currently online\"`. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "EditConflictGuard silently fails when Supabase query errors",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx",
      "description": "In useOptimisticLock, the checkConflict function returns false on error without notifying the user. If the conflict check network call fails, the user proceeds to save thinking no conflict exists, potentially overwriting another user's changes. No toast or error state is surfaced.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/EditConflictGuard.tsx, in the useOptimisticLock hook's checkConflict callback: BEFORE: When `error` is truthy from the Supabase query, the function silently returns `false`, giving a false all-clear. AFTER: When `error` is truthy, call `useUiStore.getState().addToast({ type: 'warning', title: 'Conflict check failed', message: 'Could not verify if someone else edited this item. Save with caution.' })` and return `false` but also set a new state variable `checkFailed: true` that the component can use to show a subtle warning icon. Add `checkFailed` and `setCheckFailed` via useState(false), reset it to false on successful check. Export `checkFailed` from the hook return object. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C1-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "realtime",
      "title": "EntityPresence does not subscribe to Supabase realtime for presence changes",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/EntityPresence.tsx",
      "description": "EntityPresence reads from presenceStore but never sets up a Supabase realtime subscription or Liveblocks room connection for the specific entity. The presence data shown is stale unless another component happens to update the store. Users viewing the same entity won't see each other without an active subscription.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/EntityPresence.tsx, in the PresenceDots component: BEFORE: The component only reads `usePresenceStore(s => s.getUsersViewingEntity(entityId))` without ensuring any subscription is active for that entityId. If no parent component has set up a Liveblocks room or Supabase presence channel for this entity, the viewers array is always empty. AFTER: Add a `useEffect` that calls `usePresenceStore.getState().trackEntity(entityId)` on mount and `usePresenceStore.getState().untrackEntity(entityId)` on unmount. If `trackEntity` does not exist on the store, add a comment `// TODO: presenceStore.trackEntity not yet implemented — presence data depends on parent PresenceBar subscription` and add a fallback: if viewers.length === 0, render null (already done) but also check if the store has been initialized (add a `isInitialized` selector). This ensures the component doesn't show misleading empty state when presence tracking isn't wired. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "PresenceBar avatar overflow not handled on mobile viewports",
      "file": "src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx",
      "description": "The presence bar renders all avatars in a horizontal flex row. On mobile (375px), if 8+ users are online, avatars overflow the container or get squished below the 44px touch target minimum. No max-visible limit is applied, and no responsive collapse to a compact count badge exists.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/collaboration/PresenceBar.tsx, in the main component where avatar circles are mapped and rendered: BEFORE: All online users are rendered as avatar circles without a maximum visible count. On narrow screens the row overflows. AFTER: Add a `maxVisible` constant set to 5. Render only the first `maxVisible` users as avatar circles. If there are more users than `maxVisible`, render a `+N` overflow badge (a circle with the count) styled identically to avatars but with `backgroundColor: colors.surfaceOverlay` and `color: colors.textSecondary`. The overflow badge should have `aria-label={`${overflow} more team members online`}`. Add a CSS media query or inline style check: on screens below 640px, reduce `maxVisible` to 3. Ensure all visible avatars maintain a minimum 44x44px touch target on mobile by setting `minWidth: 32` and `minHeight: 32` with adequate spacing. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "collaboration-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "typescript_quality",
      "title": "messageReactions API uses untyped number for messageId instead of string UUID",
      "file": "src/api/endpoints/messageReactions.ts",
      "description": "All three exported functions (fetchReactions, addReaction, removeReaction) accept `messageId: number` but the Supabase schema uses UUID (string) primary keys for all entity tables. This type mismatch will cause runtime query failures when passing UUID strings. The return type casts are also unsafe with `as string`.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/api/endpoints/messageReactions.ts, in all three exported functions (fetchReactions, addReaction, removeReaction): BEFORE: `messageId` parameter is typed as `number`. The response mapping uses `as string` casts on emoji and user_id. AFTER: Change `messageId: number` to `messageId: string` in all three function signatures. Remove the `as string` casts in fetchReactions's map callback and instead type the select response properly: `const { data, error } = await supabase.from('message_reactions').select('emoji, user_id').eq('message_id', messageId)`. If the table doesn't exist in the Database type, add a comment `// Table may not exist in generated types yet — using untyped query` and keep the current approach but fix the parameter type to string. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore lacks real-time presence indicators entirely — users discover conflicts only when saving. Fieldwire has basic presence but no edit locking or conflict resolution. SiteSync's EditConflictGuard with optimistic lock checking is architecturally sound and ahead of competitors, but the silent failure on network errors and missing Supabase realtime subscriptions in EntityPresence undermine the advantage. Fixing these issues would give SiteSync a genuine differentiator in concurrent editing safety that no competitor currently offers.",
  "summary": "The Collaboration & Presence module has the right architectural bones — optimistic conflict detection, presence stores, edit lock APIs, and entity-level viewer indicators. However, execution gaps prevent it from delivering on the 'real-time everything' vision. The five most impactful issues are: (1) PresenceBar avatars are completely inaccessible to screen readers and keyboard users, violating WCAG and the Global Acceptance Standards; (2) the EditConflictGuard silently swallows network errors during conflict checks, creating a false sense of safety that could lead to data overwrites; (3) EntityPresence never establishes its own realtime subscription, making presence data stale; (4) the PresenceBar overflows on mobile without any max-visible cap, breaking field-first usability; and (5) the messageReactions API uses `number` for message IDs when the schema uses UUID strings, guaranteeing runtime failures. Fixing these five issues would raise the module from 41 to approximately 58, with the accessibility and error handling fixes having the highest impact on user trust and data integrity."
}
```
